data.title: Fed meeting minutes: Hope to see further progress on inflation before cutting interest rates
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The minutes of the Federal Reserve meeting showed that the Federal Reserve hopes to see "further progress on inflation" before deciding to cut interest rates again. Citing rising inflationary pressures, the Federal Reserve stabilized interest rates in the range of 4.25%-4.5% at its last meeting, and the market expects no rate cut at the March meeting. According to the minutes of the meeting, the committee unanimously agreed that "the Federal Reserve has ample time to assess the changing prospects for economic activity, the labor market, and inflation." Many economists and market strategists worry that tariffs and stricter immigration policies could increase inflationary pressures, potentially offsetting the positive effects of lower taxes and relaxed regulations. The Federal Reserve has previously indicated that it is in no hurry to cut interest rates further due to persistently high inflation. Inflation has fallen from its post-epidemic highs, but remains stubbornly higher than the Fed's ideal level. For example, the consumer price index in January increased by 3% year-on-year, the fastest pace in seven months and higher than the Fed's 2% target. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Lightning bug allows remote theft of bitcoin via LND nodes
data.description:
A bug in relatively recent versions of Bitcoin Lightning node software LND could allow hackers to remotely drain bitcoin from victims.
The post Bitcoin Lightning bug allows remote theft of bitcoin via LND nodes appeared first on Protos.
source: https://protos.com/bitcoin-lightning-bug-allows-remote-theft-of-bitcoin-via-lnd-nodes/

data.title: SEC seeks public comments for WisdomTree’s proposal for an XRP ETF
data.description:
The U.S. SEC is looking for public comments to decide how to move forward with a proposal for an XRP exchange-traded fund.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/342239/sec-seeks-public-comments-for-wisdomtrees-proposal-for-an-xrp-etf?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: "Fed megaphone": Fed officials consider slowing or pausing balance sheet reduction
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: "Federal Reserve Mouthpiece" Nick Timiraos said that the minutes of the Federal Reserve's January meeting showed that officials discussed whether to slow down or suspend the reduction of its nearly $6.8 trillion asset portfolio at last month's meeting. They face the complex issues brought about by raising the federal debt ceiling in the coming months. Dynamics related to the debt ceiling may cause large fluctuations in the Federal Reserve's liabilities. The process of shrinking the balance sheet will eventually deplete the reserves of the banking system, and Federal Reserve officials are not sure how long this process will last. How the Treasury will manage money market fluctuations caused by its cash balance may complicate the Federal Reserve's ability to determine the correct balance of reserves. Therefore, according to the minutes of the meeting released on Wednesday, officials believed at the January meeting that "it may be appropriate to consider suspending or slowing the reduction of the balance sheet until the debt ceiling issue is resolved." (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Survey Indicates Balance Sheet Reduction To Conclude By Mid-2025
data.description: According to Odaily, respondents in a Federal Reserve survey anticipate that the process of reducing the balance sheet will conclude by mid-2025, slightly later than previously expected. The economic outlook provided by Federal Reserve staff remains largely unchanged from the projections shared during the December meeting. In initial discussions on framework review, policymakers expressed openness to revising elements introduced in the 2020 document. Additionally, Federal Reserve officials emphasized the need for caution in considering interest rate cuts due to increased uncertainty.

data.title: Fed meeting minutes: Uncertainty increases, need to "proceed with caution" in rate cuts
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Respondents to the Fed survey believe that the balance sheet reduction process will end in mid-2025, slightly later than previously expected, and the Fed staff's economic outlook remains largely unchanged from the outlook provided at the December meeting. In preliminary discussions on the framework review, policymakers expressed openness to modifying elements introduced in the 2020 document. In addition, Fed officials believe that with increased uncertainty, "caution" is needed in cutting interest rates. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed meeting minutes: Potential impact of Trump's policies raises inflation concerns
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Federal Reserve policymakers said at a meeting held about a week after Trump took office on January 20 that Trump's initial policy proposals have raised the Fed's concerns about rising inflation. According to the published minutes, participants "generally pointed out the upside risks to the inflation outlook" rather than risks to the job market. "Participants specifically mentioned the potential impact of potential changes in trade and immigration policies, the possibility of geopolitical developments disrupting supply chains, or stronger-than-expected household spending." The minutes stated that although participants remained confident that price pressures would continue to ease, "other factors were seen as likely to hinder the anti-inflation process," including "some (Federal Reserve) regional business contacts indicating that companies will try to pass on higher input costs from potential tariffs to consumers." Participants also pointed out that some indicators of inflation expectations, a key issue of concern to the Federal Reserve, "have recently risen." (Jinshi)
